key_insight: By optimizing disk packings with collective coordinates, the authors create two-dimensional polycrystals that are simultaneously ultradense, stealthy, and hyperuniform, unlike conventional polycrystals.
engineering_relevance: These microstructures could enable metamaterials with improved transparency at long wavelengths and enhanced diffusion/transport behavior for nanolithography- or 3D-printed designs.
method: Collective-coordinate optimization to generate stealthy hyperuniform (SHU) polycrystalline disk packings
Modified Lubachevsky-Stillinger rapid compression to produce nonhyperuniform reference packings (LS)
Compare structure, grain statistics, specific surface area, diffusion spreadability, and optical response
evidence_signal: The abstract reports extensive comparative analysis showing distinct grain-size distributions in SHU packings and confirms, via the nonlocal strong-contrast expansion, vanishing small-wavevector attenuation for dielectric SHU samples versus LS controls.
watch_item: These SHU polycrystals are not expected to form spontaneously, so practical use depends on precise fabrication control and may be limited by nanoscale manufacturing tolerances.
paper_title: Two-dimensional stealthy hyperuniform polycrystalline disk packings
